etymology.description: 地(地) (dì, "earth; ground") refers to the physical earth or land. 區(區) (qū, "area; district") denotes an assigned area or division. Together, 地區 (dìqū, "region; area") represents a designated section or space on the earth's surface.
etymology.interpretation: 地(地) (dì, "earth; ground") represents the physical land surface. 區(區) (qū, "area; district") refers to a specific division or portion. When combined, 地區 (dìqū, "region; area") effectively describes an expanse of land that is identified as a specific segment or region, whether defined by natural, administrative, or other boundaries.

examples.chinese: 这个地区气候温暖(這個地區氣候溫暖)。
examples.english: The climate in this region is warm.

examples.chinese: 政府正在发展这个地区的经济(政府正在發展這個地區的經濟)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèngfǔ zhèngzài fāzhǎn zhège dìqū de jīngjì.
examples.english: The government is developing the economy of this region.

examples.chinese: 不同的地区有不同的文化习俗(不同的地區有不同的文化習俗)。
examples.pinyin: Bùtóng de dìqū yǒu bùtóng de wénhuà xísú.
examples.english: Different regions have different cultural customs.
